_basis: IEC 60364-4-41:2005+AMD1:2017 Clause 411.3.3 (30mA additional protection), IEC 60364-5-53 (device selection), IEC 60364-7 series (special locations)
_note: RCDs provide protection against indirect contact (fault voltage on exposed metal parts) and additional protection against direct contact (protection against shock from a live conductor). Different IΔn ratings and types serve different purposes. This file covers when and what type of RCD is required.

RCD_types_by_current_waveform._note: RCD type selection is critical — an RCD of the wrong type may not detect certain fault current waveforms.

type_AC.does_not_detect: Pulsating DC, smooth DC, high-frequency currents
type_AC.permitted_for: Circuits supplying purely resistive or linear AC loads — basic lighting, heaters
type_AC.not_permitted_for: Circuits with EV chargers, VFDs, switched-mode power supplies, medical equipment
type_AC.note: Many older standards permitted Type AC as the default. IEC 60364-4-41 AMD1:2017 now restricts Type AC to circuits where DC components of fault current are excluded by design.

type_A.detects: Sinusoidal AC fault currents AND pulsating DC fault currents (rectified half-wave or full-wave)
type_A.does_not_detect: Smooth DC, high-frequency above rated detection bandwidth
type_A.permitted_for: General circuits including single-phase electronic equipment, luminaires with electronic ballasts, single-phase EV chargers (Mode 2/3), most domestic and commercial loads
type_A.recommended_as_standard: Type A is the minimum recommended type for general use in modern installations
type_A.note: Switch-mode power supplies and Class II EV chargers can produce pulsating DC fault currents. Type AC RCDs will not detect these — Type A is the minimum.

type_F.detects: Sinusoidal AC, pulsating DC, AND composite currents (multiple frequencies — typical of variable-speed drives)
type_F.permitted_for: Circuits supplying VFDs (variable frequency drives / inverters) for single-phase motors, washing machine motors with VFD
type_F.note: AMD1:2017 to IEC 60364-4-41 introduced Type F requirement for circuits with single-phase VFDs. Type F is more sensitive to the complex waveforms these devices produce.

type_B.detects: Sinusoidal AC, pulsating DC, smooth DC, and high-frequency fault currents
type_B.permitted_for: All circuit types including three-phase EV chargers (Mode 3 with three-phase onboard charger), three-phase VFDs, medical IT systems with DC components
type_B.mandatory_for: Three-phase EV charging points (Mode 3/4) unless a separate DC detection device (IΔn DC ≤ 6mA) is incorporated in the EVSE
type_B.note: Type B RCDs are significantly more expensive. Consider specifying a Type A RCD with a separate DC fault monitoring module for three-phase EV circuits as a cost-effective alternative where permitted.

type_S_time_delayed.description: Type S (or 'Selective') RCDs have an intentional time delay — they trip between 130ms and 500ms at IΔn and ≤ 150ms at 5× IΔn, instead of immediately (<300ms at IΔn for non-delayed types).
type_S_time_delayed.purpose: Selectivity — used upstream of 30mA RCDs so that only the downstream RCD trips on a fault, preserving supply to unaffected circuits.
type_S_time_delayed.typical_IΔn: 100 mA to 300 mA for selective function at main board
type_S_time_delayed.note: Type S must be used upstream where 30mA RCDs are installed downstream. Without type S upstream, a serious fault may trip both the 30mA and the 300mA RCD — losing entire floor or building supply.

6mA.application: DC fault detection device for EV chargers where Type B RCD is not fitted
6mA.note: A 6mA smooth DC detection device monitors for DC leakage ≥ 6mA, which would blind a Type A RCD. When triggered, it disconnects before the RCD is required to operate.

10mA.note: Not commonly used outside specialist applications

30mA.application: Additional protection against direct contact — mandatory for: socket outlets up to 20A (all accessible socket outlets used by general public or untrained persons), cables buried in walls at depth < 50mm without earthed metallic coverage, circuits in bathrooms (IEC 60364-7-701), and all circuits in locations accessible to the general public
30mA._mandatory_requirement_411_3_3: IEC 60364-4-41 AMD1:2017 Clause 411.3.3 — RCDs with IΔn ≤ 30mA are required for: (a) all socket outlets up to 20A rated current for general use; (b) cables concealed in walls or partitions at depth < 50mm that are not in earthed metallic conduit or containment.
30mA.contact_voltage_at_trip: I × Ra — at 30mA and Ra = 1667Ω (extreme), contact voltage = 50V — borderline safe
30mA.practical_contact_voltage: If Ra ≤ 200Ω (typical earthed installation), contact voltage = 0.030 × 200 = 6V — very safe

100mA.application: Fire protection — detection of sustained earth leakage that could cause smouldering fires. Not for direct contact protection. Used on circuits in high fire-risk areas.

300mA.application: Upstream protection at distribution board — fire protection and upstream fault protection. Must be Type S (time-delayed) where 30mA RCDs are installed downstream.

500mA_to_1A.application: Fire protection on large commercial circuits, protection of submains in high-leakage installations (many VFDs, long cable runs with leakage). Not for personal protection.
500mA_to_1A.note: Verify total background leakage current < 0.3 × IΔn to avoid nuisance tripping in installations with many electronic devices

mandatory_RCD_locations._reference: IEC 60364-4-41 Clause 411.3.3 and Part 7 special locations

socket_outlets_general_use.requirement: 30mA RCD protection for all socket outlets ≤ 20A intended for use by general public or untrained persons
exceptions: Socket outlets under supervision of skilled persons (e.g. in sealed electrical room only accessed by electricians)
Socket outlets in specific industrial applications where risk assessment justifies omission
Shaver sockets (supplied through isolating transformer — SELV — no RCD needed)

cables_in_walls.requirement: 30mA RCD for cables concealed in walls or partitions at depth < 50mm
cables_in_walls.exemptions: Cables in earthed metallic conduit or trunking, or cables forming part of a wiring system with earthed metallic sheath, do not require 30mA RCD under this rule

TT_earthing_systems.requirement: ALL circuits in TT systems must be RCD protected (ADS by overcurrent devices is impractical in TT systems — see part4-41-electric-shock.json)
TT_earthing_systems.IΔn: 30mA for final circuits, 300mA Type S at main board
TT_earthing_systems.note: In rural areas of Africa and Asia where TT earthing is common, every circuit panel must have RCDs on every circuit

bathrooms_IEC_60364_7_701.requirement: 30mA RCD for all circuits in the bathroom
bathrooms_IEC_60364_7_701.note: Any circuit with a cable passing through a bathroom zone requires 30mA RCD protection. Socket outlets in Zone 2 are prohibited except where specifically permitted by national code.

swimming_pools_IEC_60364_7_702.requirement: 30mA RCD for all circuits within pool zones 0, 1, and 2

construction_sites_IEC_60364_7_704.requirement: 30mA RCD for all socket outlets up to 32A. 500mA RCD for circuits > 32A.
construction_sites_IEC_60364_7_704.note: Construction sites have particularly high shock risk — damaged cables, wet conditions, unqualified users. RCD protection is non-negotiable.

agricultural_IEC_60364_7_705.requirement: 30mA RCD for all socket outlets. 300mA or 500mA for circuits feeding fixed equipment in livestock buildings.
agricultural_IEC_60364_7_705.note: Livestock are highly sensitive to small currents. IEC recommends additional precautions where animals could contact live parts.

marinas_IEC_60364_7_708.requirement: 30mA RCD for all shore supply circuits, preferably on each pedestal outlet
marinas_IEC_60364_7_708.note: Corrosive environment — increased risk of cable damage and earth fault. Individual RCD per berth socket provides best selectivity.

medical_locations_IEC_60364_7_710.Group_0: Standard installation — 30mA RCDs on socket outlets
medical_locations_IEC_60364_7_710.Group_1: 30mA RCD on all circuits, Type A minimum. No IT system requirement.
medical_locations_IEC_60364_7_710.Group_2: IT system (insulated from earth) with insulation monitoring device (IMD). No RCDs in operating theatres — the IT system prevents shock on first fault. RCDs are used in non-critical areas. 30mA RCDs on any sockets in general areas.

EV_charging_IEC_60364_7_722.requirement: RCD with IΔn ≤ 30mA required for all EV charging points

type_requirement.single_phase_Mode_2_3: Type A RCD minimum (some national codes require Type B — check local code)
type_requirement.three_phase_Mode_3: Type B RCD, or Type A + separate DC fault monitoring device (IΔn_DC ≤ 6mA)
type_requirement.note: EV onboard chargers produce DC fault currents. Type AC RCDs will not detect these and must not be used on EV circuits.

PV_systems_IEC_60364_7_712.AC_side: 30mA RCD on inverter output circuit. Type A minimum, Type B if inverter can produce DC component.
PV_systems_IEC_60364_7_712.DC_side: No RCD on DC side unless national code requires. DC circuit requires isolation and arc fault protection.
PV_systems_IEC_60364_7_712.inverter_type: Most modern grid-tied inverters have galvanic isolation — Type A RCD is sufficient. Non-isolated inverters require Type B on AC output.

S_type_trip_times.note: Type S RCDs intentionally delay to allow downstream instant-trip RCDs to operate first, maintaining supply selectivity

non_tripping_requirement.at_0_5_IΔn: RCD must NOT trip at 0.5 × IΔn (to prevent nuisance tripping from normal leakage currents)

leakage_current_and_nuisance_tripping.cause: Every electrical device with a Class I or Class II connection has some leakage current to earth through Y-capacitors, EMC filters, and cable capacitance. In aggregate, multiple devices on one circuit can reach a significant total leakage.
leakage_current_and_nuisance_tripping.rule_of_thumb: Total leakage on any circuit should be < IΔn / 3 (i.e., < 10mA for a 30mA RCD). Above this, nuisance tripping is likely.
mitigation: Use higher IΔn (100mA or 300mA) for VFD and industrial circuits — not appropriate for personal protection
Choose VFDs and equipment with lower leakage ratings
Avoid very long cables on single 30mA RCD circuits
